Off Calendar Jun-28-2023 Continued To Jul-26-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-22-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amanda Ebey appeared by zoom for Mary Anigilaje.

The court questions whether there any assets other that the $136,000.00. Mrs. Ebey responded "no.", stated that they received a bank statement showing that there is about $85,000.00 in the account, the deceased administrator (Mrs. Wilkins) was using the account as her own, and that they need to get the money to her (Mrs. Wilkins) estate which is below the probate threshold. Mrs. Ebey also stated that they decided to go with a bond rather than getting waivers from the heirs. The Petition for Letters of Administration (successor) is under submission. Bond will be set at $85,000.00. Status on close of estate is set for June 28, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mrs. Ebey is to file the bank statement, the 13100 affidavit from the nine heirs, and receipts for any distributions made to Mrs. Wilkins' heirs prior to the hearing date.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-16-2019 Continued To Feb-13-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amanda Ebey for Deana Wilkins.

Ms. Ebey apologized stating that the matter fell off her calendar and that she needs a little bit more time to meet with her Client. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to February 13, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Counsel that if the Probate Examiner's notes are not responded to and the matter is not ready to go forward, it will consider ordering the petition off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.
